Self-Organizing Map
Each cell is a neuron colored by cluster. Neurons close together share similar listing language. Click any cell to highlight that cluster.

What Each Cluster Means
LLM-generated plain-English names and descriptions for each market-language segment, derived from top TF-IDF terms.

Trajectories Through Listing-Language Space
Each line traces a census tract's movement across time periods. Background shading shows cluster regions.

Neighborhood Paths
Overall movement statistics and the cluster sequence followed by each highlighted neighborhood across the study period.
Loading…
Transition Matrix
Probability of a tract moving from one cluster (row) to another (column) between consecutive periods. The diagonal shows tracts that stayed in the same cluster.
Loading…
Trajectory Narratives
The most common cluster sequences across all tracts, with LLM-generated narratives describing what each trajectory pattern represents — gentrification, stability, decline, or something else.
